question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `validate()` calls `Sets.union(grpc.keySet(), rest.keySet()).stream().anyMatch(StringUtils::isBlank)` on an arbitrarily large map without size limits, causing the validation itself to consume significant CPU when processing thousands of scenario keys, and then proceed to spawn thousands of subscriber connections against the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `GrpcSubscriberProperties.startTime` to a timestamp just before a known high-volume period in the topic's history, causing the mirror node's retriever to replay a burst of historical messages to all active subscribers simultaneously, spiking its processing resource consumption above 30% of the 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `clients = 1024` and configure a single gRPC scenario with `subscribers = 1024`, causing `GrpcClientSDK` to create 1024 SDK clients each holding a persistent gRPC channel to the mirror node, and `GrpcSubscriber` to spawn 1024 concurrent subscription streams, increasing the mirror node's connection and stream management resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `RestSubscriberProperties.retry.maxBackoff` at its minimum (500ms) and `retry.maxAttempts = 16`, causing each failed REST API call to retry 16 times with minimal backoff, generating a burst of 16 rapid requests to the mirror node per failed transaction lookup, increasing its HTTP processing resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure the `grpc` map with many scenarios each subscribing to a different high-traffic `topicId`, causing the mirror node's gRPC listener to maintain separate polling or Redis subscription channels per topic, multiplying its per-topic resource consumption and increasing overall processing load by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `GrpcSubscriberProperties.duration` to an extremely large value (no upper bound enforced), causing each gRPC subscription to remain open indefinitely and accumulate resource consumption on the mirror node's gRPC processing threads above 30% of the 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `RestSubscriberProperties.samplePercent = 1.0` with `publishers` set to empty (matching all publishers) and `limit = 0` (unlimited), causing the REST subscriber to issue an unbounded stream of transaction verification requests to the mirror node's REST API, increasing its processing resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `GrpcSubscriberProperties` constructor setting `retry.setMaxAttempts(Long.MAX_VALUE)` to ensure that any transient gRPC error causes the monitor to immediately reconnect, generating a sustained stream of new subscription requests to the mirror node that increases its connection-setup CPU load by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `clients = 1024` with `statusFrequency = 1s`, causing `GrpcClientSDK` to maintain 1024 open SDK clients while `SubscribeMetrics.status()` iterates over all active subscriptions every second, creating a combined CPU and connection overhead on the mirror node that exceeds 30% of the 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure many gRPC scenarios each with `startTime = Instant.EPOCH` and `subscribers = 1024`, causing the mirror node's historical retriever to simultaneously serve full-history scans to 1024 * N concurrent streams, driving its database query and network egress resource consumption above 30% of the 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `RestSubscriberProperties.timeout` to 500ms (minimum) across many REST scenarios, causing the mirror node's REST API to receive a high rate of requests that each time out before completion, forcing the server to handle both the initial request processing and the connection teardown overhead, increasing its resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure the `grpc` map with the maximum number of unique scenario names (no map size limit enforced), each with `subscribers = 1024` and `startTime = Instant.EPOCH`, causing the mirror node to simultaneously serve historical message streams to thousands of concurrent gRPC subscribers, increasing its CPU and I/O resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `GrpcSubscriberProperties.retry.maxBackoff` defaults to only 8 seconds, causing the monitor to reconnect to the mirror node at most every 8 seconds per subscriber after failures, and with 1024 subscribers this generates up to 128 reconnection attempts per second against the mirror node, increasing its resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `RestSubscriberProperties` with `samplePercent = 1.0`, `timeout = 500ms`, and `retry.maxAttempts = 16` across many REST scenarios, causing each published transaction to trigger up to 16 * N rapid REST API calls to the mirror node, increasing its HTTP handler CPU and connection resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `GrpcSubscriberProperties.startTime` to a timestamp far in the past and `limit = 0` (unlimited), causing `GrpcSubscription.getTopicMessageQuery()` to set `limit = 0` which maps to unlimited messages, forcing the mirror node to stream the entire topic history without bound and sustaining its resource consumption above 30% of baseline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure many gRPC scenarios each targeting a different valid `topicId` with `subscribers = 1024`, causing the mirror node's gRPC service to maintain separate subscription state for each topic-subscriber combination, exhausting its per-subscription memory and CPU resources above 30% of the 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `clients = 1024` and configure many REST scenarios with `samplePercent = 1.0`, causing the REST subscriber to issue transaction verification requests distributed across 1024 SDK clients, saturating the mirror node's REST API connection pool and increasing its resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `GrpcSubscriberProperties` with `retry.minBackoff = 500ms` and `retry.maxBackoff = 8s` combined with a `topicId` that causes intermittent errors (not `INVALID_ARGUMENT`), causing `GrpcSubscriber.shouldRetry()` to always return true and trigger continuous reconnection cycles against the mirror node, increasing its resource consumption by over 30%?
